title: Tangy Ukrainian Barley Pilaf
description: A hearty and flavorful Ukrainian pilaf dish made with barley, tangy ingredients, and a blend of spices.

ingredients.name: Barley
ingredients.notes: Use short-grain barley for best results.

ingredients.name: Tangy Vinegar
ingredients.notes: White wine vinegar or apple cider vinegar work well.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Use a good quality olive oil for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion finely for best results.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced garlic adds a nice flavor to the pilaf.

ingredients.name: Dried Thyme
ingredients.notes: Fresh thyme is also great if you have it.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Black Pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper is always best.

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the barley and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the tangy vinegar, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the barley is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
